title: jasminum nudiflorum f. pulvinatum
intro: Jasminum nudiflorum f. pulvinatum is a variant of the Winter Jasmine plant, characterized by its deciduous, twining vines with yellow flowers that bloom from late winter to early spring. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in China, where its flowers and leaves are valued for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Jasminum nudiflorum f. pulvinatum are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and promote digestion, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
